# Design Tokens

Design tokens are the single source of truth for all visual properties in Terra UI. They define colors, spacing, typography, and other design decisions in a format that components consume at runtime.

## Architecture

### Two layers

```
Raw tokens (flat files)  →  Theme object (single-level map per category)
packages/ui/src/theme/tokens/*.ts     packages/ui/src/theme/theme.ts
```

**Raw token files** are flat objects with dotted string keys. They are the source of record — expected to be generated by Figma export or a design-sync agent. Do not hand-edit them during normal development.

**The theme object** groups tokens by their top-level category (`color`, `spacing`, `radius`, etc.). Each category is a **single-level flat map** — the dotted key is preserved as a string key in the object. There is intentionally no deep nesting.

```ts
// Raw token file (flat)
export const light = {
  'color.surface.default': '#ffffff',
  'color.surface.raised': '#ffffff',
  'color.action.bg.primary': '#009966',
  'color.action.bg.primary.hover': '#007a55',
};

// Theme object (single-level per category)
theme.color['surface.default']           // '#ffffff'
theme.color['action.bg.primary']         // '#009966'
theme.color['action.bg.primary.hover']   // '#007a55'
```

This avoids deeply nested objects, keeps TypeScript types flat and predictable, and makes token lookup a simple string key access.

---

## File structure

```
packages/ui/src/theme/
├── tokens/
│   ├── primitives.ts   ← palette ramps, spacing scale, radius, typography
│   ├── light.ts        ← light-scheme semantic tokens  (generated)
│   └── dark.ts         ← dark-scheme semantic tokens   (generated)
├── types.ts            ← TypeScript interfaces for the theme object
└── theme.ts            ← assembles raw tokens → theme object consumed by unistyles
```

`primitives.ts` holds raw values (hex colors, dp numbers). `light.ts` and `dark.ts` reference those primitives using `{palette.key}` notation — resolved at build time.

---

## Naming convention

```
[category].[context].[property?].[variant].[state]
```

Each segment is required only when relevant. Omit trailing segments rather than leaving them empty.

| Segment | Description | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **category** | Token type | `color`, `spacing`, `radius`, `font` |
| **context** | Where it is used | `surface`, `action`, `text`, `border`, `status` |
| **property** | The visual attribute — **only when the context has more than one** | `bg`, `fg`, `border` |
| **variant** | Weight, scale, or role | `primary`, `subtle`, `strong`, `muted`, `raised`, `lg`, `md` |
| **state** | Interaction or scheme | `hover`, `active`, `disabled`, `dark` |

### When to include `property`

`property` is only needed to disambiguate when a context carries **multiple visual attributes**. When a context has a single implied attribute, omit it — the context already makes it clear.

| Context | Implied attribute | Include `property`? |
|---|---|---|
| `text` | foreground color | No — text is always a foreground |
| `surface` | background color | No — surfaces are always backgrounds |
| `border` | border color | No — borders are always border-color |
| `action` | both `bg` and `fg` | **Yes** — needs disambiguation |
| `status` | `bg`, `fg`, and `border` | **Yes** — needs disambiguation |

### Rules

- Use `kebab-case` within a segment (e.g., `on-accent`, `on-success`).
- All segments use lowercase.
- Never skip a middle segment — if you need a state, all segments before it must be present.
- States describe UI interaction (`hover`, `active`, `disabled`) or scheme (`dark`). Do not encode component names as states.

---

## Examples by category

### `color`

```
color.surface.default             ← default page/card background
color.surface.raised              ← elevated surface (sheet, card)
color.surface.sunken              ← recessed surface (input, well)
color.surface.overlay             ← modal scrim

color.action.bg.primary           ← primary fill (default)
color.action.bg.primary.hover     ← primary fill on hover
color.action.bg.primary.active    ← primary fill on press
color.action.bg.primary.disabled  ← primary fill when disabled
color.action.fg.primary           ← primary label/icon color
color.action.bg.subtle            ← tinted/ghost fill
color.action.bg.subtle.hover
color.action.fg.subtle

color.text.default                ← body text
color.text.muted                  ← secondary text
color.text.subtle                 ← tertiary / placeholder text
color.text.disabled
color.text.inverse                ← text on dark surface
color.text.accent                 ← brand-colored text on neutral bg
color.text.on-accent              ← text on solid accent surface
color.text.on-accent-subtle       ← text on tinted accent surface

color.border.default              ← standard divider
color.border.subtle               ← de-emphasized border
color.border.strong               ← emphasized border
color.border.focus                ← keyboard focus ring

color.status.bg.success           ← success fill (solid)
color.status.bg.success.subtle    ← success fill (tinted)
color.status.border.success       ← success border (solid context)
color.status.border.success.subtle ← success border (subtle context)
color.status.fg.success           ← text on status.bg.success
color.status.fg.success.subtle    ← text on status.bg.success.subtle
```

### `spacing`

```
spacing.1     ← 4dp
spacing.2     ← 8dp
spacing.4     ← 16dp
spacing.screen.margin.x
spacing.screen.margin.y
```

### `radius`

```
radius.base   ← authored lg/×1 step; full scale derived from this
radius.sm
radius.md
radius.lg
radius.full
```

### `font`

```
font.size.body-md
font.weight.semibold
font.family.regular
font.line-height.body-md
```

---

## Accessing tokens in components

Components consume the theme object via `react-native-unistyles`. The category is accessed as a property; the rest of the token path is a flat string key.

```ts
import { StyleSheet } from 'react-native-unistyles';

const styles = StyleSheet.create((theme) => ({
  container: {
    backgroundColor: theme.color['surface.default'],
    borderColor: theme.color['border.default'],
    borderRadius: theme.radius['md'],
  },
  button: {
    backgroundColor: theme.color['action.bg.primary'],
    color: theme.color['action.fg.primary'],
  },
}));
```

Use bracket notation (`theme.color['action.bg.primary']`) — dot-chained access will not work for keys containing dots.

---

## Reference notation in raw token files

Semantic token values may reference a primitive by wrapping its key in `{}`:

```ts
// light.ts
'color.action.bg.primary': '{palette.emerald.600}',  // resolves to '#009966'
'color.action.bg.primary.hover': '{palette.emerald.700}',
```

References are resolved against `primitives.ts` at theme-build time. Raw hex is used only when no palette step matches exactly.

---

## Token generation

`light.ts` and `dark.ts` are generated files. The expected workflow is:

1. Design tokens are authored in Figma.
2. A Figma export plugin or design-sync agent writes `light.ts` and `dark.ts`.
3. `primitives.ts` is also generated but changes infrequently (palette ramps are stable).

Do not edit `light.ts` or `dark.ts` manually unless making a deliberate one-off patch pending the next export. Commit any manual edits with a note explaining why they diverge from the Figma source.
